【Day 10】Lend Me Your USB! I Need to Play Warcraft!
Code Review of My Youth - From the BIOS Beep to Software Engineering

Hit the 4GB limit when copying to USB? Starting from a Warcraft-class memory, this post covers format levels, FAT32 limits, and the differences between NTFS and exFAT — using a story to introduce the basics of file systems.
【Day 9】Why Can't My File Open? Turns Out It's the Extension!

Files won't open? Often the issue is the extension. Starting from an instant-messenger memory, this post explains what extensions do, common misunderstandings, and hidden risks — helping you intuitively understand the relationship between file types and security.
【Day 8】Submitted Your Homework Yet? Hurry Up and Burn It to a CD!

Back when USB drives weren't common, our main way to store data was optical discs — CDs, VCDs, DVDs. The discs looked like frisbees, round with a reflective coating. People often used them as frisbees too, since they looked the part.
【Day 7】Turns Out URLs Aren't Magic! Behind DNS Is a Whole Lot of Lookup Work

URLs aren't magic. Starting from nslookup, this post walks through Domain vs. IP, DNS forwarding / caching / resolution paths, and the essentials of domain buying, renewal, TLDs, and WHOIS privacy — so the "type a URL → get an IP" process becomes clearer.
【Day 6】Driven Mad by the 'Little Mouse' Signing Up for Email! The @ Symbol and Ray Tomlinson

My sign-up saga with the "little mouse" — the history and use of @, the username@domain format, DNS, and how social-network @ mentions extend the same idea. Untangling why Email looks the way it does.
【Day 5】Wrecked on My First CS Match! From Internet Café to Understanding IPs

In elementary school, a classmate dragged me into an internet café and I played CS for the first time — and got wrecked. From not knowing how to connect, to my first time hearing "type in the IP", this is the lesson that took me from single-player into the networked world.
【Day 4】Argh! Why Is the Screen Blue and White?! My BSoD and BIOS Memoir
Have you ever had that moment where your computer just freezes, the screen turns blue and white, and you sit there in shock?
【Day 3】Maze on the Motherboard — The First Time I Opened the Case

My dad brought home a computer from his office and my first personal computer was born. From hype to total confusion to it breaking down — I opened the case for the first time and entered the mysterious world called the "motherboard". I started to understand what's inside a computer: CPU, RAM, hard drive, PSU. Every piece was a starting point for my future engineering life.
【Day 2】Hooked on the First Login — Walking into the Web through Slime's First Home

Early on, the computer was just "a box that opens up to play games" to me. Until one day I ran into a really grumpy older cousin who showed me something on the screen that wasn't a game — a website called "Slime's First Home". That's where my internet world began.
【Day 1】The First 'Beep' — Where My Computer World Begins

Do you remember the first time you touched a computer? For me, it was a memory mixed with mystery, taboo, and pure excitement. From the first "beep" of a boot sequence to learning to open MS-DOS, run games, and tweak config.sys — I went from a kid hooked on computers to walking the road to becoming a software engineer.
